2604.06865.title: Physical Adversarial Attacks on AI Surveillance Systems:Detection, Tracking, and Visible--Infrared Evasion
2604.06865.paper_summary: Physical adversarial attacks are increasingly studied in settings that resemble deployed surveillance systems rather than isolated image benchmarks. In these settings, person detection, multi-object tracking, visible--infrared sensing, and the practical form of the attack carrier all matter at once. This changes how the literature should be read. A perturbation that suppresses a detector in one frame may have limited practical effect if identity is recovered over time; an RGB-only result may say little about night-time systems that rely on visible and thermal inputs together; and a conspicuous patch can imply a different threat model from a wearable or selectively activated carrier. This paper reviews physical attacks from that surveillance-oriented viewpoint. Rather than attempting a complete catalogue of all physical attacks in computer vision, we focus on the technical questions that become central in surveillance: temporal persistence, sensing modality, carrier realism, and system-level objective. We organize prior work through a four-part taxonomy and discuss how recent results on multi-object tracking, dual-modal visible--infrared evasion, and controllable clothing reflect a broader change in the field. We also summarize evaluation practices and unresolved gaps, including distance robustness, camera-pipeline variation, identity-level metrics, and activation-aware testing. The resulting picture is that surveillance robustness cannot be judged reliably from isolated per-frame benchmarks alone; it has to be examined as a system problem unfolding over time, across sensors, and under realistic physical deployment constraints.
2604.06865.paper_summary_zh: 物理對抗攻擊在類似於實際部署的監控系統的環境中越來越受到研究，而不是孤立的圖像基準。在這些環境中，人物檢測、多物體追蹤、可見--紅外感測以及攻擊載體的實際形式都同時重要。這改變了文獻應該如何被解讀。在某一幀中抑制檢測器的擾動，如果身份隨時間恢復，可能會有有限的實際效果；僅僅依賴RGB的結果可能對依賴可見和熱輸入的夜間系統幾乎沒有意義；而一個顯眼的補丁可能暗示著與可穿戴或選擇性啟動載體不同的威脅模型。本文從監控導向的觀點回顧物理攻擊。我們不試圖對計算機視覺中的所有物理攻擊進行完整的目錄，而是專注於在監控中變得中心的技術問題：時間持續性、感測模態、載體真實性和系統級目標。我們通過四部分的分類法組織先前的工作，並討論近期在多物體追蹤、雙模態可見--紅外逃避和可控服裝方面的結果如何反映該領域的更廣泛變化。我們還總結了評估實踐和未解決的空白，包括距離穩健性、攝影機管道變化、身份級指標和啟動感知測試。結果的圖景是，監控的穩健性不能僅僅從孤立的逐幀基準中可靠地判斷；它必須作為一個系統問題隨時間展開，跨越感測器，並在現實的物理部署限制下進行檢查。
2604.06865.author: Miguel A. DelaCruz et.al.
2604.06865.authors: Miguel A. DelaCruz, Patricia Mae Santos, Rafael T. Navarro
